claim_boundary: places persistent observer-facing record objects into the observer-derived modular-response H3 chart using only sampled observers that see the object. The preview receipt requires either a localized H3 object subpopulation beating shuffled incidence or a whole-population compactness distribution that robustly beats shuffled incidence, plus the strict modular-response H3 chart gate. The displayed localized H3 precursor is not by itself a bulk-population proof: the final bulk-population receipt still requires either a localized non-boundary subpopulation that beats its matched shuffled controls, or a robust whole-population compactness distribution with the non-boundary leakage audit passing. This is not a neutral third-person reconstruction, CMB, or particle claim. This is a postprocessed object-chart recomputation from saved observer JSONL and a cached H3 report; it does not rerun dynamics and does not add evidence beyond the supplied run and H3-refit receipt.
